name: Protected page PageShell and DataState normalization
description: Refactor eight protected frontend route surfaces so they follow the canonical PageShell/DataState route pattern from docs/FRONTEND.md. The work standardizes title ownership, shell padding, and blocking async state presentation across settings, expense trash, group detail, budgets, groups, insights, onboarding, and home empty-state flows without changing backend contracts, store semantics, or product behavior.

planned: Research audit completed across the eight target protected pages plus the key budget/group/insights child components.

GitNexus upstream impact checks for SettingsPage, ExpenseTrashPage, GroupDetailPage, BudgetsPage, GroupsPage, InsightsPage, OnboardingPage, and OverviewPage all returned LOW risk with 0 affected processes.
Plans index records feat-067 as active and scheduled in three implementation batches.

notes: Frontend-only orchestration refactor. DataState should be used for blocking card-shaped states where it fits, but specialized Insights and Onboarding layouts may keep custom async markup when a generic card would reduce clarity.
